feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: FWTR056 Error During NDF Transaction Creation in FTR_CREATE
entry.content:
Hello,

 

We are encountering error FWTR056 while attempting to create an NDF transaction using transaction FTR_CREATE.

 

We have performed a detailed review of the configuration, including product type, transaction type, and all relevant customizing settings, and no inconsistencies were identified.

 

The issue occurs consistently during transaction creation.

 

For your analysis, we have attached:

• A detailed step-by-step document outlining how the error is reproduced

• Screenshots and configuration details related to the product and transaction setup

 

At this point, we suspect there may be a missing dependency, configuration inconsistency not visible at first level, or a system-related issue.

 

Could you please assist us in identifying the root cause of error FWTR056 and advise on the appropriate resolution?

 

If additional information is required, we will be happy to provide it.

 

Thank you for your support.

 

Best regards,


--- Support Assistant ---
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: None of these


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Define Long Text Control for Order Types Configuration is missing
entry.content:
Hi,

I am looking for the reply for this url 

 

Thanks,

entry.title: https://me.sap.com/notes/2571755
entry.content:
We would like to influence the default Business Partner role and the Business Partner role drop down in the BP transaction. We found this oss note https://me.sap.com/notes/2571755 but it's not applicable for our version SAP_ABA    75I    0003 (SAPK-75I03INSAPABA).

What is the corresponding oss note for our version

entry.title: Activation of Default Communication Method (DFLT_COMM) Field in BP
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team,

We would like to seek your guidance on activating the Default Communication Method field in the Business Partner (BP) transaction under Address → Communication.

Field details:

  • Field name: DFLT_COMM
  • Structure: ADDR1_DATA
  • Data Element: AD_COMM
  • Framework: Business Partner (BDT) / Address Services

Requirement:
Our business would like to maintain the Default Communication Method directly in the BP UI (BP transaction → Address → Communication section) and make it available for manual maintenance or controlled derivation.

Analysis Performed

We have performed the following checks and analysis:

  1. The field DFLT_COMM exists in structure ADDR1_DATA and is populated internally by Address Services.
  2. The field is not visible in the standard BP UI, and it does not appear in standard:
    • BDT screen groups
    • Field groups
    • SPRO field control settings for BP
  3. We reviewed several SAP blogs and documents describi

entry.title: How to fix Backend error in ISLM scenario: SD_INT_AI_GPT in Joule?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

When I ask Joule questions related to sales, it returns the error:
‘Backend error in ISLM scenario: SD_INT_AI_GPT. Check the intelligent scenario maintenance in transaction ISLM_CONN_MAP.’

When I then go into S/4HANA and run transaction ISLM_CONN_MAP, I see another error — ‘HTTP failure, processing failed, invalid state, invalid timeout, or others’ — when I click Check Connection.

 

Has anyone encountered a similar issue before, or have any suggestions on how to troubleshoot this?

(Some of my questions are: Has the goods of sales order 12345678 arrived? When was the sales order 12345678 created?)

(I am integrating Joule with S/4HANA PCE.)

Any tips or guidance would be highly appreciated. Thank you in advance!

Joule SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Enable ssl in Hana replication for DR
entry.content:
Hello ,

Could you please help me in implementing the best practice of SSL activation for HANA DB replication with DR site?

Regards;

entry.title: sost mail queue empty in Test system
entry.content: As we can see there is no emails triggered or present under sost Tcode. Kindly check

entry.title: Need All Characters in Uppercase in DMEE Payment File (PAYM – /ZGA_CGI_XML_CT_CITI)
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We require all alphanumeric characters in the payment file to appear in capital letters, as highlighted in the attached example.

We attempted to achieve this using the conversion function CLU in the DMEE tree, however, the system is generating an incorrect payment file after applying this function.

Request your guidance on how we can correctly convert the values to uppercase within the DMEE format tree:

  • Tree Type: PAYM
  • Format Tree: /ZGA_CGI_XML_CT_CITI

Could you please advise which conversion function is required.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Regards,
Gokul Sawant

entry.title: How to post negative sales in S4
entry.content:
We have a business scenario where in POS negative sale is allowed, but in S4 it is not. So when posting WPUUMS idoc system should post the negative sale in a specific storage location and upon the stock arrival it should automatically transfer the stock from main location to this specific storage location to settle the negative balance. This ensures sales WPUUMS and payment WPUTAB gets settled.

How to achieve this, if anyone can help.

entry.title: SAP GUI UPGRADE
entry.content:
We are planning to upgrade SAP GUI - windows 32.
could you provide the below details,
1.The latest SAP GUI version Available and its recommended Patch level?
2.Key advantages and improvements in the latest version?
3. Is there any known limitations or drawbacks?


--- Support Assistant ---
Q: Please categorize which SAP Frontend Product you use
A: SAP GUI for Windows
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: SAP GUI Usability and settings


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Unable to Change Posting Date for Physical Inventory in Difference Analyzer - Embedded EWM
entry.content:
Currently it is impossible to set or change the posting date for Physical Inventory Documents. You can define the document date and the counting date, but none of them impact the posting date of the PI document. Whenever we post the difference to ERP side "Analyze Difference" the posting date field is filled with the current date and is unchangeable. 

--- Steps to Reproduce ---

  1. Create a PI document in /SCWM/PI_CREATE
  2. Go to edit mode on PI document and do the "Count" on back date and "Post" the count in EWM and save.
  3. Open the Difference Analyzer app /SCWM/DIFF_ANALYZER
  4. Unable to change the posting date

How to influence the posting date in ERP?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - API based on OAuth
entry.content:
There are lots of relevant help document and comprehensive blog post on this topic. I just try to summerize the process.

1. Create a system user. Tcode SU01

2. Enable OAuth for the OData API

This is for OData v2

tcode: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE

 

This is for OData v4

tcode: /IWFND/V4_ADMIN

select the project, then select service line, then click on the oauth button. check the flag in the popup dialog. 

3. Maintain OAuth2 Client ID for the system user

tcode: SOAUTH2

This is a view maintained

Please copy the token endpoint URL generated in the view. 

For the client authentication section, please select SSL Client Certificate as well, since the 3rd party client system might not want to use basic auth to retrieve the token. 

4. Maintain Scope for the Client ID

Still in the same view, go to the section Scope Assignment. You can add the scope ID to the table list. Please be aware that only the APIs whose OAuth has been enabled have the scope ID to be selected here. If you cannot find the scope ID in the search help,  please check the configuration in step 2.

 

5. Test in POSTMAN

This is the step retrieving OAuth2 token

This is the step using the token for consuming the API

6. Can CPI consume S4 OData by using OAuth authentication?

With current version, it is not possible. There are two main restrictions.

Restriction 1: with current version(1.26) of the OData v2 receiver adapter, It doesn’t support OAuth.

With current version(1.18) of the OData V4 receiver adapter for on premise OData Service, it doesn’t support OAuth

Restriction 2: with current version of the HTTPS receiver adapter, it supports OAuth client credentials.

However, the OAuth Client Credential configuration view inside the Security Material section, doesn’t provide an option for On Premise Token URL via the cloud connector.

The token URL hostname of S4 cannot be recognised by CPI.

 

Conclusion

SAP API does support OAuth2 Authentication. You should try to avoid using basic auth for exposing these APIs.

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - SOAP Inbound web service on the client certificate
entry.content:
The below information involves

  • private key/certificate generation
  • certificate/user mapping in S4
  • certificate maintain in S4
  • SOAMANAGER configuration in S4

Normally the first three tasks are done by SAP Basis and the last step is taking care by SAP Integration developers. Please make sure you have relevant skillset and access in the system before go through the document.

Prerequisit

install openssl. This is the tool used to generated private key and self signed certificate.,

1. Switch on ‘Certificate Mapping Rule’ in S4

TCODE: RZ11

Parameter: login/certificate_mapping_rulebased

Make sure the value is 1. If it is default value 0, change it to 1.

2. Create a system user

TCODE: SU01

Make sure the user has enough roles

3. Generate Relevant Self Signed Certificate and Private Key

Of course, it doesn't have to be a self signed certificate. You can have the certificate signed by a Certificate Authority(CA) for the certificate of your firm. Normally you need to provide information of your firm to the CA and the sign process is not free of charge.

For our case, we use a free self signed certificate and no need to involve any CAs.

Step 1. Create a private key

openssl genrsa -out private.key 2048

Step 2. Generate self signed certificate

openssl req -new -x509 -key private.key -out client.crt -days 365

here is an example. The custom value has been highlighted.

  • Country Name (2 letter code) [AU]:AU
  • State or Province Name (full name) [Some-State]:Victoria
  • Locality Name (eg, city) []:Melbourne
  • Organization Name (eg, company) [Internet Widgits Pty Ltd]:Google
  • Organizational Unit Name (eg, section) []:IT
  • Common Name (e.g. server FQDN or YOUR name) []:TEST01
  • Email Address []:TEST01@GOOGLE.COM

This is a screen shot of OpenSSL

Just make CN value as the user id created above

now we have two files:

  • private.key
  • client.crt

4. Configure Certificate and User Mapping

TCODE: CERTRULE

Click change button, then click the button to import the client certificate. 

Click rule button

map CN with username

 

This is the result after saving

5. Import client certificate to STRUST

in order to be able to generate a successful connection, the S4 server certificate should be imported to the client system normally. Otherwise, the client system might not trust S4 endpoint exposed. This process is nothing to do with the steps above.

TCODE: STRUST

Click the change button. Then click view ‘SSL server Standard’. Then click button Import Certificate

 

this is the certificate uploaded

click save button

6. Unit Test

6.1 Expose a SOAP service

Let's take BusinessPartnerERPByIDQueryResponse_In for test.

TCODE: SOAMANAGER

just enable X.509 Client Certificate

get the access URL

 

 

 

6.2 Setup POSTMAN

Get hostname.

TCODE: SMICM. Get the hostname:port

then import the private key and certificate into POSTMAN

For testing,  you should form a request message similar like this:

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<soap:Envelope xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/">
    <soap:Body>
        <n0:BusinessPartnerERPByIDQuery_sync xmlns:n0="http://sap.com/xi/APPL/Global2">
            <MessageHeader>
                <CreationDateTime/>
            </MessageHeader>
            <BusinessPartnerSelectionByID>
                <InternalID>IC0003</InternalID>
            </BusinessPartnerSelectionByID>
        </n0:BusinessPartnerERPByIDQuery_sync>
    </soap:Body>
</soap:Envelope>

send the requst without authentication and you will get the response the S4 server.

 

Conclusion.

SAP SOAP based inbound web service supports client certificate.

entry.title: S/4 hana data anonymize
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m currently looking for ways to anonymize data on SAP S/4hana quality system after refresh,
did you have any idea how to proceed?
Thanks

entry.title: F17P cannot consolidate balance reports across multiple company codes (Ford Blue, Pro, and Model-e).
entry.content:
Currently, Ford utilizes three distinct company codes to represent its business segments: Ford Blue, Ford Pro, and Ford Model-e. Many customers conduct business across all three segments simultaneously.

When the Balance Confirmation process is executed using T-code F17P specifically for Poland and CZECH, the system generates individual reports restricted to a single company code. This results in fragmented reporting for a single customer entity. There is a business requirement to provide these customers with a single, unified balance confirmation statement that aggregates their financial position across all relevant Ford company codes, rather than receiving three separate documents.

Solution Needed Expert guidance is requested from the SAP support teams on the following:

  1. Standard SAP Functionality: Is there a standard configuration or parameter within F17P (or related programs like RFKORB00/RFDORB00) that allows  to generate consolidated report for multiple company code for a single customer?
  2. BTE (Business Transaction Events): Can a specific BTE (such as those in the 1310 or 1320 se

entry.title: F17P cannot consolidate balance reports across multiple company codes (Ford Blue, Pro, and Model-e).
entry.content:
Currently, Ford utilizes three distinct company codes to represent its business segments: Ford Blue, Ford Pro, and Ford Model-e. Many customers conduct business across all three segments simultaneously.

When the Balance Confirmation process is executed using T-code F17P specifically for Poland and CZECH, the system generates individual reports restricted to a single company code. This results in fragmented reporting for a single customer entity. There is a business requirement to provide these customers with a single, unified balance confirmation statement that aggregates their financial position across all relevant Ford company codes, rather than receiving three separate documents.

Solution Needed Expert guidance is requested from the SAP support teams on the following:

  1. Standard SAP Functionality: Is there a standard configuration or parameter within F17P (or related programs like RFKORB00/RFDORB00) that allows  to generate consolidated report for multiple company code for a single customer?
  2. BTE (Business Transaction Events): Can a specific BTE will help resolve this issue?

entry.title: White font in SAP Belize Deep Theme in Fiori
entry.content: In Fiori, while using SAP Belize deep theme, white font is getting displayed unless we click or hover over it. Users never had this issue previously with the same theme

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - RFC Destination on OAuth2
entry.content:
In this situation, SAP S4 works as a client which consumes a service exposed by other service provider system.

Prerequisite

Before configruation, you will get a service key from the service provider including a client id/client secret/token url similar like this

{
  "private_key": "-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----\nMIIEvQIBADANBgkqhkiG9w0BAQEFAASCBKcwggSjAgEAAoIBAQDJ... (actual private key) ...\n-----END PRIVATE KEY-----\n", 
  "client_id": "123456789012345678901", 
  "token_uri": "https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token" 
}

Configuration

1. Create a Client Profile

TCODE: SE80

for instance, if the client is to consume a Mulesoft API, we can name it ZMULE as below

2. Configure Profile

TCODE: SOAUTH2_CLIENT

click create button

Select the client profile created just now and input a client id from the service key you got from the service provider.

in our example, the client id in the Prequisite section is '123456789012345678901'.

then fill in the token url

in the next tab fill in the client secret

save the configuration.

3. Unit Test

The easist way to conduct a connectivity test is via RFC Destination

TCODE: SM59

Get a working endpoint from the service provider and fill the URL into the RFC destination with type G

In the Logon & Security tab, select the client profile configured in the last step.

in the security section, activate SSL and select the view containing the trusted server certificate of service provider system.

click on button 'Connection Test'

you will get a view like this showing that authentication is working. On the other hand, you need to check in the service provider system if any incoming traffic.

Once the RFC destiantion is ready, you can use it as a building block in 

  • Intergation server configuration in SXMB_ADM, or
  • Service Consumer configuration in SOAMANAGER manually, or
  • HTTP_CLIENT in ABAP, etc

 

However, By hard coding the URL, ABAP can still implement an http client consuming a service, similar like this

DATA: lo_http_client TYPE REF TO if_http_client,
      lv_url         TYPE string VALUE 'https://example.com',
      lv_response    TYPE string.

cl_http_client=>create_by_url(
  EXPORTING
    url                = lv_url
  IMPORTING
    client             = lo_http_client
  EXCEPTIONS
    argument_not_found = 1
    plugin_not_active  = 2
    internal_error     = 3
    OTHERS             = 4 ).

However this is not a recommended way. The connection information is better to be configurable so that when the content has been transported into susequential systems like QA, Pre-Prod or Prod, it would be more flexible to update

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - RFC Destination on Client Certificate
entry.content:
In this case, SAP S/4 HANA works as a client. It consumes service from provider system. 

Prerequisite

install openssl

here are the steps for configuration

1. Generate Private key by openssl

openssl genrsa -out private.key 2048

2. Generate a certificate by openssl

openssl req -new -x509 -key private.key -out client.crt -days 365

here is an example. The custom value has been highlighted.

  • Country Name (2 letter code) [AU]:AU
  • State or Province Name (full name) [Some-State]:Victoria
  • Locality Name (eg, city) []:Melbourne
  • Organization Name (eg, company) [Internet Widgits Pty Ltd]:ServiceProvider
  • Organizational Unit Name (eg, section) []:IT
  • Common Name (e.g. server FQDN or YOUR name) []:TEST
  • Email Address []:

3. Merge key and certificate into .pfx by openssl

Since SAP doesn't support import private key directly, we can only merge the key file and certificate file into a .pfx file , then import the .pfx file to SAP S4.

openssl pkcs12 -export -out client.pfx -inkey private.key -in client.crt

 4. Upload .pfx into a new PSE in S4

TCODE: STRUST

you maybe need to create a new PSE at first

create a new SSL client identity

import the .pfx file

input the password for accessing the file

import certificate.

after the .pfx file has been imported, double click on the PSE, double click on the subject on the right, the certificate information will be shown in the view below. click the button 'add to cert. list'. The certificate will be placed in the list.

5. Unit Test

since SAP S4 is working as a client, we will create a RFC Destination type G to check the connection.

TCODE: SM59

Create a RFC destination with type G. Fill in the service URL information.

in the Logon & Security tab, check Active option for SSL and in the dropdown menu of SSL Client PSE ID, choose the one created just now.

save the RFC destination and conduct a connection test.

you maybe get an error response like this. as long as the information is coming from the service provider system, it means the authentication is working.

Once the RFC Destination has been configured, it can be used as a building block in many places like:

  • Service Consumer in  SOAMANAGER
  • Integration Engine for middleware like CPI

Conclusion

RFC Destination does support client certificate

entry.title: S/4 HANA How to Get rid of Basic Auth - SOAMANAGER Manual Configuration on Oauth2
entry.content:
In this case, SAP S4 HANA works as a client (service consumer) . The service provider does support OAuth2 authentication.

Prerequisite

an OAuth2 Client Profile should be configured. Please refer to here for detail steps.

Configuration in SOAMANAGER

TCODE: SOAMANAGER

click on the 'web service configuration'

input an outbound interface, say SalesOrderNotification_Out

Create a logical port for this outbound interface. in the step 2, consumer security, select OAuth2.0 and choose the client profile configured just now

The rest of configuration is the same as the steps for normal web service configuration.
